seoTitle: How to Integrate Freshdesk With Your Website
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Freshdesk to your website so visitors can submit support tickets or chat directly from your pages.
intro: Integrating Freshdesk with your website gives visitors a direct path to support without leaving your site. This guide covers the general steps for connecting Freshdesk's tools to your web pages.

steps.heading: Choose your integration method
steps.body: Decide whether you want to embed a contact form, a widget, or a live chat feature on your website.

steps.heading: Generate the integration code
steps.body: In your Freshdesk admin settings, generate the embed code or snippet for the feature you selected.

steps.heading: Add the code to your website
steps.body: Paste the code into your website's HTML, typically in the header or footer so it loads on every page.

steps.heading: Test the integration
steps.body: Visit your live site and confirm the widget or form appears and successfully creates tickets in Freshdesk.

faq.question: Do I need coding experience to integrate Freshdesk?
faq.answer: Basic integration usually just requires pasting a provided code snippet into your site, though some customization may need HTML familiarity.

faq.question: Can I integrate Freshdesk with any website platform?
faq.answer: Freshdesk's embed code works with most standard website platforms that allow custom HTML.

faq.question: Will the integration slow down my website?
faq.answer: Freshdesk's widgets are designed to load asynchronously, minimizing impact on your site's performance.

faq.question: Can I track tickets submitted from my website?
faq.answer: Yes, tickets submitted through your integrated form or widget appear in your Freshdesk dashboard like any other ticket.
